yaml.title: Proposed Solutions
yaml.subtitle: Closing the 51.3% unrecognized gap — a prioritised roadmap

markdown.markdown:
```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import polars as pl import plotly.graph_objects as go CSV = '/Users/omkarmalpure/Documents/broadjump_v2/analyze-data/UOM-38552.csv' df = pl.read_csv(CSV, null_values=['', 'NULL']) unrecog_df = df.filter(pl.col('IsRecognized') == False) total = len(df) unrecog = len(unrecog_df) no_ph = len(unrecog_df.filter(pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') == 0)) low_ph = len(unrecog_df.filter((pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') > 0) & (pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') < 5))) some_ph = len(unrecog_df.filter(pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') >= 5)) FONT = 'Inter, sans-serif' SFONT = 'Space Grotesk, sans-serif' ``` ```{python} #| echo: false fig_u = go.Figure(go.Pie( labels=['No/Zero Purchase History', 'Very Low History (1–4 units)', 'Price Out of DBSCAN Range'], values=[no_ph, low_ph, some_ph], marker=dict( colors=['#ef4444', '#f97316', '#f59e0b'], line=dict(color='white', width=3), ), textinfo='label+percent', textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=11), insidetextorientation='radial', hole=0.5, hovertemplate='%{label}
%{value:,} rows
%{percent}', )) fig_u.add_annotation( text=f'{unrecog:,}
Unrecognized', x=0.5, y=0.5, showarrow=False, font=dict(family=SFONT, size=13, color='#0f172a'), align='center', ) fig_u.update_layout( paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='white', font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155'), legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=-0.18, xanchor='center', x=0.5, font=dict(size=11), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)'), margin=dict(t=20, b=80, l=20, r=20), height=380, showlegend=True, ) fig_u.show() ``` | Root Cause | Rows Affected | Proposed Strategy | |:-----------|:-------------:|:------------------| | No / zero purchase history | `{python} f"{no_ph:,}"` | Solution A — Agentic web search | | Very low history (1–4 units) | `{python} f"{low_ph:,}"` | Solution B — Similar-product transfer | | Price outside DBSCAN range | `{python} f"{some_ph:,}"` | Solution C — Threshold relaxation | --- ## Solution A — Agentic Web Search
🤖
Solution A · Zero-History Products
Agentic Web Search with Citation
Targets `{python} f"{no_ph:,}"` rows with no purchase history

When a product has no purchase history at all, Step 5 has no anchors. The only signal available is the product description, manufacturer, and catalog number. An agentic pipeline can autonomously enrich these rows:

  • Query construction — build a structured search from RecognizedProductDesc, RecognizedManufacturerName, and RecognizedProductCatalogNumber.
  • Search & extract — agent calls a web search tool or medical-device catalogue API and extracts candidate UOM labels and typical pack sizes.
  • Citation attachment — every suggestion is stored with its source URL so analysts can verify before accepting.
  • Confidence flagging — agent rates its own confidence ("exact catalog number match" vs. "description keyword match"). Low-confidence results require mandatory analyst sign-off before write-back.
  • Feedback loop — accepted suggestions are written to purchase history so future runs resolve the same products without a search.
High effort Requires analyst review Self-improving over time Needs prompt fine-tuning
--- ## Solution B — Similar-Product Transfer
🔗
Solution B · Sparse History Products
Purchase History Bridge via Product Similarity
Targets `{python} f"{low_ph:,}"` rows with 1–4 unit history

Rather than going to the web, look inside existing purchase history for structurally similar products and borrow their ThresholdV2 clusters as a prior:

  • Similarity candidates — find products in the catalogue sharing the same RecognizedManufacturerID, same RecognizedProductTypeID, and similar description tokens (keyword overlap or embedding distance).
  • UOM profile transfer — the similar product's ThresholdV2 clusters and WAP are borrowed. The proximity scoring in Step 5 runs exactly as normal, using the borrowed thresholds.
  • Downgraded confidence — the resulting score is multiplied by a transfer discount factor (e.g., 0.80) to signal the assignment is inferred, not directly observed.
  • Analyst review queue — transferred rows surface in a separate queue for analysts to confirm the analogy is valid before the result propagates.
Medium effort Needs similarity criteria co-design with analysts No external API calls
### Key questions for analysts Before implementing, these must be answered collaboratively: - Does same **manufacturer + product type** constitute a strong enough analogy, or are there product categories where this would be systematically misleading? - What **discount factor** appropriately penalises borrowed confidence? - Are there clinical categories (implants vs. consumables) that require different similarity criteria? --- ## Solution C — Threshold Relaxation
⚙️
Solution C · Price Out-of-Range Products
Widen the Proximity Window + Analyst Escalation
Targets `{python} f"{some_ph:,}"` rows with history but score < 0.30

These rows have adequate purchase history but the file's PriceUOM lands too far from every DBSCAN cluster. Common causes: supplier price changes, new pack sizes, or unit vs. case mismatches. Two complementary approaches:

  • Widen LOG_EPS — raising LOG_EPS from 0.25 → 0.40 extends the tolerance from ~1.78× to ~2.51× price deviation. This is a global change; must be validated on a hold-out set to measure false-positive impact before deployment.
  • Price-trend adjustment — for products with time-stamped history, detect upward/downward price trends and project cluster ranges forward. A product trending $10→$13→$16 is likely ~$19 today; the 12-month-old cluster misses it.
  • Analyst escalation queue — rows where the best candidate scores 0.20–0.30 (just below acceptance) are surfaced to analysts with the best DBSCAN candidate and distance from the cluster edge visible. Fast accept/reject with feedback loops back to threshold calibration.
C1: Low effort (config change) C3: Low effort (UI change) C2: Medium effort
--- ## Coverage Comparison ```{python} #| echo: false sol_labels = [ 'C3 — Escalation Queue
Near-miss rows, score 0.20–0.30', 'C1 — Widen LOG_EPS
Config change + hold-out validation', 'B — Similar-Product Transfer
Sparse history bridge', 'A — Agentic Web Search
Zero-history enrichment', ] sol_values = [int(some_ph*0.6), int(some_ph*0.4), low_ph, no_ph] sol_colors = ['#10b981', '#06b6d4', '#4f46e5', '#8b5cf6'] effort_map = ['Low', 'Low', 'Medium', 'High'] fig_s = go.Figure() fig_s.add_trace(go.Bar( y=sol_labels, x=sol_values, orientation='h', marker=dict(color=sol_colors, line=dict(width=0)), text=[f' ~{v:,} rows' for v in sol_values], textposition='outside', textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=11, color='#475569'), hovertemplate='%{y}
~%{x:,} rows potentially recovered', cliponaxis=False, )) fig_s.update_layout( paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa', font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155', size=11), xaxis=dict( gridcolor='#f1f5f9', linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11), range=[0, max(sol_values) * 1.22], title='Estimated Rows Recoverable', ), yaxis=dict( linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(family=FONT, size=11, color='#1e293b'), autorange='reversed', title='', ), margin=dict(t=20, b=50, l=260, r=110), bargap=0.35, height=340, hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor='#0f172a', font_color='white', font_family=FONT), ) fig_s.show() ``` --- ## Recommended Roadmap
Medium Term
B — Similar-Product Transfer
Co-design similarity criteria with domain analysts. Pilot on one manufacturer/category before expanding. Borrow ThresholdV2 profiles from structurally similar products with rich history.
Longer Term
A — Agentic Web Search
Prototype on a small batch of zero-history products. Evaluate citation quality and build the analyst review workflow. Requires prompt engineering, de-duplication, and a write-back feedback loop before scaling.
| Solution | Reach | Effort | Analyst Input Needed | |:---------|------:|:------:|:--------------------:| | B — Similar-product transfer | `{python} f"~{low_ph:,}"` rows | Medium | Yes — criteria definition | | A — Agentic search | `{python} f"~{no_ph:,}"` rows | High | Yes — citation sign-off |
